title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 02
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment relates to:
questions.solution: Panchayati Raj

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What does HTML stand for?
questions.solution: HyperText Markup Language

questions.solution: Sundarbans Tiger Reserve

questions.solution: Ramananda

questions.solution: Shooting

questions.solution: 26 November 1949

questions.solution: SECL

questions.stem: Gondwana coalfields of India are found mainly in:
questions.solution: All of the above

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.solution: 25 years

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: 'Arthashastra' was written by:
questions.solution: Chanakya (Kautilya)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.solution: Perovskite solar cell

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's coal stock at power plants as of April 2026 was sufficient for:
questions.solution: 18 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Dandi March was led by:
questions.solution: Mahatma Gandhi

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Finance Commission is appointed every:
questions.solution: 5 years

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: Coking coal is primarily used in which industry?
questions.solution: Steel manufacturing

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Mysore Palace is the residence of:
questions.solution: Wadiyar dynasty

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which river basin has the largest coal deposits in India?
questions.solution: Mahanadi

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man saves 15% of income. If income rises 20% and savings rise 25%, the increase in expenditure percent is:

questions.solution: 12 days

questions.solution: 4.96 years

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains start at same time from A and B 500 km apart at 60 km/h and 40 km/h. Meeting distance from A:
questions.solution: 300 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A cone, hemisphere, and cylinder have same base radius and height. Ratio of volumes:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a skewed distribution, mean = 50, median = 48. The distribution is:
questions.solution: Positively skewed

questions.stem: If 3^(x+2) = 729, then the value of 4^(x-2) is:

questions.stem: A coin tossed 3 times. Probability at least 2 heads:

questions.stem: A vessel of 80 L, milk and water ratio 7:3. How much milk to add to make 9:1?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two taps fill in 15 and 25 min. An outlet empties at 8 L/min. Tank fills in 12.5 min. Capacity:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two numbers are in ratio 5:6. If their HCF is 4, their LCM is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A runs twice as fast as B who runs thrice as fast as C. If C covers 3 km in 30 min, A speed:
questions.solution: 36 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The value of (0.3̅ + 0.4̅ + 0.5̅) in fractional form is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A, B, C together complete in 10 days. A alone in 25, B alone in 30. C alone in:
questions.solution: 37.5 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: How many numbers between 200 and 600 are divisible by 13?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If selling price is doubled, profit triples. Profit percent was:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 30% of (x - y) = 15% of (x + y), then x : y is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man takes 6 h for a 60 km journey partly by car (80 km/h) and rest by cycle (12 km/h). Distance by cycle:
questions.solution: 18 km

questions.stem: If TO = 2020, FOR = 61818, then FORT = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A grid has 3 horizontal and 4 vertical lines. Number of parallelograms:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should CIL adopt automated mining equipment in all underground mines? Arg I: Yes, it improves worker safety. Arg II: No, it leads to job losses.
questions.solution: Both are strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Bar chart: exports 2022=$400M, 2023=$480M, 2024=$576M. Avg annual growth:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A's father is B. C is the mother of D. D is the brother of A. How is C related to B?
questions.solution: Wife

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In code, 'red apple' = 41, 'green apple' = 52, 'red grape' = 43. Code for 'green' is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Number of diagonals in an octagon:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should mine workers be given annual health check-ups mandatory? Arg I: Yes, early detection saves lives. Arg II: No, it is an additional cost for companies.
questions.solution: Only I is strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pie chart shows A=144°, B=108°, C=72°, D=36°. If total is ₹50,000, A's share:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the brother of B. B is the wife of C. C is the son of D. E is the mother of A. How is D related to E?
questions.solution: Cannot be determined

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Foreword : Preface :: Afterword : ?
questions.solution: Epilogue

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If DRILL = 4912912, BLAST = 21211920, then CORE = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Two concentric circles with radii 5 and 3. How many tangents can be drawn to both circles?
questions.solution: 2 direct + 2 transverse

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should first aid training be mandatory for all mine workers? Arg I: Yes, it saves lives in emergencies. Arg II: No, it takes time away from production.
questions.solution: Only I is strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A bar graph shows coal production: 2019=600MT, 2020=630MT, 2021=660MT, 2022=720MT. % increase 2021-22:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: P is the son of Q. R is the daughter of Q. S is the brother of P. How is S related to R?
questions.solution: Brother

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A team must have at least 2 women. From 6 men and 4 women, how many 5-person teams?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: 1, 1, 2, 6, 24, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: The government has increased the import duty on coal. Conclusion I: Domestic coal production will increase. II: Coal prices will fall.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should India invest more in underground coal gasification? Arg I: Yes, it will unlock deep coal reserves. Arg II: No, it requires advanced technology.
questions.solution: Only I is strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: From point A, B is 5 km north. C is 10 km east of B. D is 5 km south of C. How far A to D?
questions.solution: 10 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: CY, DW, EU, FS, ?
questions.solution: GQ

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong term: 7, 12, 22, 42, 82, 162, 322

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: No document is confidential. Some confidential are classified. All classified are secret. Conclusions: I. Some documents are classified. II. Some secrets are confidential.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of LETHARGIC:
questions.solution: Energetic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: project / the / completed / was / time / on
options: The project was completed on time
Was the project completed on time
Completed was the project on time
On time the project was completed
questions.solution: The project was completed on time

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of FORTIFY:
questions.solution: Weaken

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: education / the / to / key / is / success
options: Education is the key to success
To success the key is education
Success is the key to education
questions.solution: Education is the key to success

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of SPURIOUS:
questions.solution: Genuine

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: workers / demanding / are / wages / better / for
options: Workers are demanding for better wages
For better wages workers are demanding
Demanding are workers better for wages
Better wages for workers are demanding
questions.solution: Workers are demanding for better wages

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PRUDENT:
questions.solution: Reckless

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: automation / loss / leads / to / jobs / of
options: Leads to loss of jobs automation
Loss of jobs automation leads to
Automation leads to loss of jobs
Jobs loss of to leads automation
questions.solution: Automation leads to loss of jobs

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PRAGMATIC:
questions.solution: Idealistic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: quality / compromising / without / costs / reduce / should
options: Costs should reduce without compromising quality
Quality compromising without reduce costs should
Compromising quality without costs should reduce
Should reduce costs without compromising quality
questions.solution: Costs should reduce without compromising quality

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of INTREPID:
questions.solution: Cowardly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: government / measures / safety / implemented / has / stringent
options: Stringent safety measures government has implemented
Government has implemented stringent safety measures
Safety measures stringent implemented has government
Implemented has government stringent safety measures
questions.solution: Government has implemented stringent safety measures

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of CONCISE:
questions.solution: Verbose

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: renewable / future / energy / is / the
questions.solution: Renewable energy is the future

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of COPIOUS:
questions.solution: Scarce

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: team / successfully / the / completed / project / the
options: Successfully the team completed the project
The team successfully completed the project
The project successfully completed the team
Completed the project the team successfully
questions.solution: The team successfully completed the project

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERIPHERAL:
questions.solution: Central

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The mineral reserves ____ estimated at 500 million tonnes.
questions.solution: are

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of RESILIENT:
questions.solution: Tough

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is proud ____ her achievements.
questions.solution: of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of SURREPTITIOUS:
questions.solution: Open

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To take with a grain of salt':
questions.solution: To be skeptical

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of COMPLACENT:
questions.solution: Dissatisfied

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To steal someone's thunder':
options: To take credit for another's idea
questions.solution: To take credit for another's idea

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of FACILITATE:
questions.solution: Hinder

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vitrinite reflectance Ro% rank indicator: Ro < 0.5% is:
questions.solution: Lignite: Ro<0.5%. Sub-bit: 0.5-0.6%. Bituminous: 0.6-2.0%. Anthracite: >2.0%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ridge push and slab pull forces: which is dominant in plate motion?
questions.solution: Slab pull > ridge pull. Plates attached to subducting slabs move faster (Pacific).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Oceanic crust age increases:
questions.solution: Seafloor spreading: new crust at ridge, older further away.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fission track dating:
options: Spontaneous fission of ²³⁸U → damage trails in crystal. Track length T-dependent. Apatite: annealing ~110°C
questions.solution: Fission track: thermochronometer. Apatite (AFT): ~60-120°C. Zircon (ZFT): ~200-300°C.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Radon gas in buildings originates from:
questions.solution: Radon: 222Rn from uranium decay in rocks/soil. Enters buildings through cracks. Second leading cause of lung cancer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well logging — resistivity:
options: Formation resistivity (ohm-m). Water vs hydrocarbon (low vs high Rt). LWD, wireline
questions.solution: Rt vs Rxo (invaded zone). Archie: Sw = (a·Rw)/(ϕ^m·Rt)^{1/n}. Deep (LLd) vs shallow (LLs).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Diagenetic quartz cement in sandstone increases with:
questions.solution: Quartz cement: pressure solution and silica migration. Increases with T (>70°C). Major porosity destroyer in sandstones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbonate grain types: ooids, peloids, bioclasts, intraclasts. Ooids form in:
options: Warm, shallow, agitated marine water (high energy)
questions.solution: Ooids: spherical, concentric layers. Form in warm, shallow, agitated water. Bahama Banks type.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Fault breccia forms in:
questions.solution: Fault breccia: angular fragments in fault zone. Forms in brittle regime (< ~10 km depth). Cataclasite is finer-grained equivalent.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cumulate texture:
options: Early crystals accumulated by settling in magma chamber. Layering
questions.solution: Cumulates: bottom of magma chamber. Chromite layers in Bushveld Complex.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Al₂SiO₅ polymorphism — triple point:
options: Andalusite, kyanite, sillimanite. T ~500°C, P ~4 kbar. Kyanite: high P. Andalusite: low P. Sillimanite: high T
questions.solution: Triple point: all three coexist. Ky → Sil at ~4 kbar, ~550°C. And → Sil at ~550°C, <4 kbar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kriging:
options: Geostatistical interpolation. Variogram model (spherical, exponential, Gaussian). BLUE estimate
questions.solution: Kriging: semivariogram γ(h). Nugget, sill, range. Ordinary kriging: local mean unknown.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facies model — barrier island:
options: Barrier (beach, dune), inlet, lagoon, tidal delta. Transgressive (retreating) or regressive (prograding)
questions.solution: Barrier island: mixed-energy coast. Washover fan: overwash during storms. Inlet migration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of staurolite:
questions.solution: Staurolite — cruciform twins. Index mineral for medium-grade metamorphism.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A porphyritic texture (large crystals in a fine groundmass) indicates:
options: Two stages of cooling — slow then fast
questions.solution: Early slow cooling grows phenocrysts; later rapid cooling forms the fine matrix.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As coal rank increases from lignite to anthracite, the volatile matter and moisture generally:
questions.solution: Higher rank means more fixed carbon and less moisture/volatiles, raising calorific value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hotspot tracks:
options: Linear age progression — plume track. Hawaii-Emperor bend (~47 Ma). Plate motion direction change
questions.solution: Hotspot: K-Ar/track ages of islands. Pacific plate motion direction change at 47 Ma.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Rift valley formation occurs at:
questions.solution: Continental rift: extension thins crust. East African Rift.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ichnology — trace fossils:
options: Skolithos (vertical, shallow marine), Nereites (deep sea), Cruziana (shallow marine). Behavioral classification
questions.solution: Trace fossils: tracks, burrows, trails. Seilacher facies: changes with water depth. Zoophycos.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Ediacaran biota represents:
questions.solution: Ediacaran: soft-bodied organisms ~575-539 Ma. Dickinsonia, Kimberella. Precambrian, pre-Cambrian explosion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ore deposit classification — Lindgren:
options: Hypothermal (high T, deep), Mesothermal (medium T), Epithermal (low T, shallow), Telethermal (very low T)
questions.solution: Lindgren: based on T and depth. Hypo: cassiterite, W. Meso: Au-quartz. Epi: Ag-Au.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Oxygen isotope ratio in ice cores indicates:
questions.solution: Ice core δ18O: more negative = colder. Direct record of past temperature. Greenland and Antarctic cores provide 800kyr record.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slaty cleavage:
options: Planar fabric in low-grade metamorphic rocks. Perpendicular to compression
questions.solution: Slaty cleavage: aligned micas. Rock splits along cleavage. Axial planar to folds.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 105 g has a volume of 30 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 105/30 = 3.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Igneous rocks are formed by the:
options: Cooling and solidification of magma or lava
questions.solution: Igneous rocks crystallize from molten material (magma below ground, lava above).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Kimberlite pipe indicators for diamond exploration:
options: Indicator minerals such as G3G4 garnet and chromite
questions.solution: Indicator minerals: Cr-pyrope garnet, chrome diopside, chromite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aravalli Supergroup:
options: Paleoproterozoic. Metamorphosed volcano-sedimentary sequence. Pb-Zn at Zawar
questions.solution: Aravalli: 2.2-1.8 Ga. Unconformably overlies Banded Gneissic Complex. UPb dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cycle of erosion (Davis):
options: Youth → Maturity → Old age. Uplift → rapid incision → graded → peneplain
questions.solution: Davis: time-dependent. Peneplain: low relief, near base level. Rejuvenation by uplift.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shear zone:
options: Zone of ductile deformation. Mylonite, S-C fabrics, shear sense indicators
questions.solution: Shear zones: Porphyroclasts (σ, δ), mica fish. Sense: S-C fabrics indicate shear direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gutenberg discontinuity:
questions.solution: CMB: Vp drops from 13.7 to 8.1 km/s. S-wave shadow zone. Liquid outer core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bouguer slab correction formula: 2*pi*G*rho*h
options: Approximately 0.0419*rho*h mGal (h in meters, rho in g/cm3)
questions.solution: Correction = 2*pi*G*rho*h. G=6.67e-11. For rho=2.67, h=100m: ~11.2 mGal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Indian Stratigraphy — Archean:
options: Dharwar Craton (3.4-2.5 Ga), Singhbhum, Bastar, Bundelkhand
questions.solution: Dharwar: Sargur (older) and Dharwar Supergroup (younger). Greenstone belts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Alkali feldspar granite vs syenite:
questions.solution: Syenite: low quartz. Granite: >20% quartz. Both are felsic plutonic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dynamic compaction: heavy weight (10-40t) dropped from 10-30m. Effective depth approximately:
options: √(WH) meters (W in tons, H in meters)
questions.solution: Effective depth ≈ 0.5-0.8√(WH). For 20t from 20m: d ≈ 0.6×20 = 12m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock mass rating (RMR): RMR = UCS+RQD+spacing+condition+groundwater. For granite with UCS=120MPa, RQD=80%, spacing=1m:
questions.solution: UCS=120→12, RQD=80→18, spacing=1m→20, condition→20, GW→15. Total ~85. Depends on exact ratings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ground improvement:
options: Compaction, grouting, soil nailing, stone columns, deep mixing, wick drains
questions.solution: Ground improvement: increase strength, reduce settlement, improve liquefaction resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Swelling ground in tunnels:
options: Expansive minerals (smectite) → volume increase. Invert slab required
questions.solution: Swelling: reaction with water. Anhydrite → gypsum expansion. Montmorillonite in clay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Retaining wall types:
options: Gravity, cantilever, counterfort, anchored, soldier pile, sheet pile, diaphragm wall
questions.solution: Retaining wall: resist lateral earth pressure. Drainage critical. Tieback anchors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adakite: high-Sr/Y, La/Yb. Source:
questions.solution: Adakite: slab melt signature. High Sr (plagioclase absent), high Y and Yb (garnet present).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Puranas (Proterozoic) basins:
options: Cuddapah, Vindhyan, Delhi, Aravalli, Chattisgarh, Kaladgi, Bhima, Penganga
questions.solution: Purana: 'ancient'. Stable shelf deposits. Mostly undeformed except Delhi fold belt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ripple marks:
options: Asymmetric (current) or symmetric (oscillation wave). Top of bed indicator
questions.solution: Ripple marks: preserved on bed surfaces. Crest orientation perpendicular to current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phyllite vs schist:
options: Phyllite: fine-grained, silky sheen. Schist: coarse-grained, visible mica
questions.solution: Phyllite: gradational between slate and schist. Schist: foliated, >50% platy minerals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic reflection:
options: Subsurface imaging by reflected P-waves. CMP stacking, migration. Resolution: λ/4 (~20m at 50 Hz)
questions.solution: Reflection: petroleum exploration. 2D, 3D seismic. Vibroseis (land), airgun (marine).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an ore, the valueless minerals associated with the ore mineral are called:
questions.solution: Gangue is the commercially worthless material that must be separated from the ore mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Luminescence dating (OSL, TL):
options: Burial age of sediment. Light-sensitive trapped charge in quartz/feldspar. Last exposure to sunlight
questions.solution: OSL: optical stimulated. TL: thermal. Range: few years to ~500 ka. SAR protocol.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Buttress unconformity:
options: Strata onlap against topographic high (older rock). In sedimentary basins
questions.solution: Buttress: onlap onto pre-existing relief. Common in basin margins. Progressive onlap.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.6. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 6M m²×6×1.4×0.6 ≈ 30.2 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Saltwater-freshwater interface: Ghyben-Herzberg relation:
options: z = (ρ_f/(ρ_s-ρ_f))·h ≈ 40h for h above sea level

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Clay mineral identification by XRD:
options: Ethylene glycol treatment expands smectite. K-saturation collapses vermiculite
questions.solution: Smectite: expands with glycol (d₀₀₁ 17Å). Illite: no change (10Å). Kaolinite: disappears with K-sat.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Colluvium vs alluvium:
options: Colluvium: gravity-deposited, angular, poorly sorted. Alluvium: water-deposited, rounded, sorted
questions.solution: Colluvium: talus, debris flow deposits. Alluvium: river deposits. Diagnostic features: sorting, rounding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spaceborne sensors — Landsat:
options: Landsat 8: OLI (9 bands, 30m) + TIRS (2 thermal, 100m). 16-day revisit. 185km swath
questions.solution: Landsat: 1972-2023. OLI bands: coastal, blue, green, red, NIR, SWIR1, SWIR2, pan (15m), cirrus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pressuremeter test:
options: In-situ stress-strain behavior. Modulus, limit pressure, creep pressure
questions.solution: Pressuremeter: expandable probe in borehole. Ménard pressuremeter. P_0, P_f, P_l.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mantle plumes and hotspots:
options: Fixed mantle upwellings. Hawaii, Yellowstone, Reunion, Iceland. Age progression of volcanism
questions.solution: Hotspot: stationary relative to moving plate. Track: seamount chain (Emperor-Hawaii).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Principle of superposition:
options: In undeformed strata, oldest at bottom, youngest at top
questions.solution: Steno's law. Basis of relative dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Condensed section (glauconitic):
options: Maximum flooding surface. Very slow sedimentation. Rich in glauconite, phosphorite, organic matter
questions.solution: Condensed: starved shelf. MFS. Thin but time-rich. Key correlation surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphism definition:
options: Solid-state change in rock due to T/P change. No melting
questions.solution: Metamorphism: new minerals + textures. Grade: low (zeolite) → high (granulite).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a station the S-wave arrives 10 s after the P-wave. Using ~8 km per second of S–P delay, the epicentral distance is about:
questions.solution: distance ≈ (S−P time) × 8 km/s = 10×8 = 80 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SEDEX (sedimentary exhalative):
options: Zn-Pb-Ag deposits in sedimentary basins. Exhalative on seafloor. Broken Hill, McArthur River
questions.solution: SEDEX: laminated ore. Reducing basin. Also large tonnage. Bagalkot (India).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 1 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 4.5 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 1×4.5 = 4.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fracture in rock along which there is no appreciable displacement is a:
questions.solution: Joints are cracks without relative movement of the blocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+20+15) = 61%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Steady flow to well (Thiem):
questions.solution: Thiem: equilibrium. Assumptions: homogeneous, isotropic, fully penetrating, steady state.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Drainage density:
options: Total stream length / basin area. High Dd = impermeable rock, high relief, low infiltration
questions.solution: Dd < 5 km/km² (coarse) to > 500 (fine). Lithology and climate control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Limestone as engineering material:
options: Good aggregate. Karst cavities → foundation hazard. Soluble in water
questions.solution: Limestone: karst terrain. Sinkholes, caverns. Site investigation critical before construction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principle of original horizontality states that sediments are originally deposited:
questions.solution: Tilted/folded strata indicate later deformation, since beds start horizontal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Marble:
questions.solution: Marble: coarse, interlocking calcite. Pure white (Carrara). Impurities → colors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A nonconformity is an unconformity in which sedimentary rocks overlie:
questions.solution: A nonconformity has stratified rocks resting on eroded crystalline basement; an angular unconformity has tilted strata below.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Augite belongs to:
questions.solution: Pyroxene: single chain, 90° cleavage. Augite: common in basalt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Grouting: primary vs secondary holes:
options: Primary: initial spacing. Secondary: fill between primaries. Tertiary if needed
questions.solution: Split spacing grouting: primary holes → check coverage → secondary → tertiary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Retrograde metamorphism:
options: Re-equilibration during cooling/exhumation. Hydration reactions (chlorite after garnet)
questions.solution: Retrograde: hydration, often incomplete. Greenschist overprint on amphibolite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loess-paleosol sequences:
options: Loess = glacial period (cold, dry). Paleosol = interglacial (warm, wet). Climate proxy
questions.solution: Loess: wind-blown silt. Paleosol: weathered layer. China Loess Plateau: 2.6Ma record.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bouma sequence (complete):
options: Ta (massive/graded) → Tb (parallel laminated) → Tc (cross-laminated) → Td (laminated silt) → Te (pelagic mud)
questions.solution: Bouma: turbidite sequence. Ta: graded. Tb: plane parallel. Tc: ripples. Td: laminated. Te: pelagic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Karst subsidence types:
options: Cover subsidence, cover collapse, sinkhole, uvala, polje
questions.solution: Cover collapse: sudden. Cover subsidence: gradual. Sinkhole: dissolution at surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using a simplified proxy where calorific value ≈ 70 × fixed-carbon%, a coal with 50% fixed carbon has a calorific value of about:
questions.solution: CV ≈ 70 × 50 = 3500 kcal/kg (illustrative proxy).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal bed methane (CBM) desorption isotherm:
questions.solution: Langmuir: adsorption on surface. V_L = maximum gas. P_L = pressure at half V_L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock body that can store and transmit usable amounts of groundwater is a(n):
questions.solution: An aquifer is porous and permeable enough to yield water to wells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 10 m over a horizontal distance of 20 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(10/20) ≈ 26.6°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Columnar joints:
options: Polygonal columns in lava flows (cooling contraction). Basalt
questions.solution: Columnar: perpendicular to cooling surface. Giant's Causeway, Devil's Tower.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Serpentine:
options: Mg3Si2O5(OH)4. Alteration of olivine. Asbestos (chrysotile). Slippery surface
questions.solution: Serpentine: hydrous Mg silicate. Serpentinite: rock. Lizardite, antigorite, chrysotile.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Infiltration capacity:
options: Maximum rate at which water enters soil. Controlled by rainfall intensity vs soil conductivity
questions.solution: Horton: f = f_c + (f_0 - f_c)e^(-kt). Decays to f_c (saturated conductivity).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.6. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 6M m²×4×1.4×0.6 ≈ 20.2 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault — scarp:
options: Fault scarp: topographic expression of fault. Degraded by erosion
questions.solution: Fault scarp: free face. Triangular facets along normal fault range fronts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of galena:
questions.solution: Galena (PbS) — metallic grey, cubic cleavage. Main lead ore.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 45° shows a width of 30 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 30×sin45° ≈ 21.21 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ductile shear zone — S-C fabrics:
options: S-foliation (flattening), C-foliation (shear). Angle indicates shear sense
questions.solution: S-C: S (45° to shear), C (parallel). Type I (S-C mylonites). Type II (S-C' in S-C mylonites).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slug test:
options: Instantaneous change in water level. For low K aquifers. Hvorslev, Bouwer-Rice
questions.solution: Slug test: quick, inexpensive. T estimate for monitoring wells. Near-well conditions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with surface (opencast) mining, underground coal mining is generally chosen when the seam is:
options: Deep, so that the stripping ratio becomes uneconomic
questions.solution: When overburden-to-coal (stripping) ratio is too high for opencast, underground methods become economic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chromite ore genesis in ophiolites:
options: Podiform: mantle section. Stratiform: layered intrusion. Cr/(Cr+Al) indicates tectonic setting
questions.solution: Podiform: mantle, harzburgite host. Stratiform: layered mafic intrusion. Cr#: higher = more depleted mantle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spheroidal weathering:
options: Corners of joint blocks rounded by chemical attack. Concentric shells
questions.solution: Spheroidal: in jointed rocks. Water penetrates joints, attacks from all sides. Corestones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydrological modeling in GIS:
options: Flow accumulation, stream network, watershed delineation. Sink filling required
questions.solution: DEM → fill sinks → flow direction (D8) → flow accumulation → stream threshold → watershed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assimilation and fractional crystallization (AFC):
options: Country rock assimilation + fractional crystallization. Combined effect. DePaolo equations
questions.solution: AFC: r = assimilation rate / crystallization rate. Trace element + isotope modeling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 10% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geotechnical baseline report (GBR):
options: Defines baseline ground conditions for contractual purposes
questions.solution: GBR: contractual baseline. Used in dispute resolution. FIDIC conditions of contract.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Turbidite Bouma sequence division B:
questions.solution: Bouma B: upper parallel lamination. Upper flow regime. Graded bedding often present. Part of complete turbidite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of magnetite:
questions.solution: Magnetite (Fe3O4) — strongly magnetic. Black streak. Important iron ore.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Birefringence is:
questions.solution: Birefringence: n_epsilon - n_omega. Measured on universal stage.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Landfill leachate contains:
questions.solution: Leachate: water percolating through waste picks up contaminants. Requires liner and collection system. Contaminates groundwater.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of talc on Mohs scale:
questions.solution: Talc (Mg3Si4O10(OH)2) — softest mineral. Diamond = 10.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GPS (Global Positioning System): minimum 4 satellites for:
questions.solution: 4 satellites: solve for 4 unknowns (x,y,z,dt). More satellites improve geometry (DOP).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Lineation in metamorphic rocks indicates:
questions.solution: Stretching lineation: alignment of elongate minerals. Indicates transport/stretching direction. Key kinematic indicator.
